template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Tummy Time for Newborns and Infants
diagnosis_name: Tummy Time Guidance for Newborns and Infants
condition: Tummy Time Guidance for Newborns and Infants

associated_conditions_to_prevent: Poor neck and upper body strength

indications: All healthy newborns and infants (while awake and supervised)
Improvement of neck and upper body strength

non_pharmacological_advice: Start tummy time as soon as baby is medically stable (first few days of life)
Begin with short sessions (1–2 minutes), 2–3 times daily
By 3 months: aim for total 30–60 minutes/day (divided sessions)
Always ensure baby is awake and supervised
Place baby on a firm, clean, flat surface
Engage baby with face-to-face interaction, talking, singing, or toys
Alternate head position during sleep (back sleeping only)
Encourage upright holding and minimize prolonged time in car seats/swings

positions_for_tummy_time: On caregiver’s chest or lap (early weeks)
Supported on tummy time pillow (short duration only)

developmental_benefits.visual: Improves visual tracking and head control

red_flags: No attempt to lift head by 2 months
Excessive crying or cyanosis during tummy time
precautions: Never leave baby unattended during tummy time
Avoid tummy time immediately after feeding
Stop if baby shows distress and retry later gradually
parent_counseling: Tummy time is safe when supervised — निगरानी में पेट के बल लेटना सुरक्षित है
Initial fussiness is normal; gradual introduction helps — शुरू में रोना सामान्य है; धीरे-धीरे आदत बनाएं
Consistency is key for development — नियमितता विकास के लिए ज़रूरी है
Helps prevent flat head and supports early milestones — सिर चपटा होने से बचाता है और विकास में मदद करता है
Make tummy time interactive and enjoyable — खेल-खेल में करें, मज़ेदार बनाएं
Avoid forcing if baby is very distressed — बहुत रोए तो ज़बरदस्ती न करें

common_myths_corrected.fact: Safe when baby is awake and supervised

common_myths_corrected.myth: Baby cries so tummy time should be skipped

common_myths_corrected.fact: Can also be done on chest, lap, or knees

follow_up.interval: Routine well-baby visit or earlier if concerns
refer_if: Poor head control beyond expected age

opd_checklist: Explained when to start tummy time?
warning_signs: No attempt to lift head by 2 months — 2 महीने तक सिर उठाने की कोशिश न करना
Persistent head lag beyond 3-4 months — 3-4 महीने बाद भी सिर पीछे गिरना
Asymmetrical movement or head preference — एक तरफ़ सिर रखने की आदत या असमान हलचल
Excessive stiffness or floppiness — ज़्यादा अकड़न या ढीलापन
Delayed motor milestones — विकास के पड़ाव देर से पूरे होना
expected_course: Start tummy time from first few days of life with short 1-2 minute sessions. By 3 months, aim for 30-60 minutes total per day in divided sessions. Most babies become comfortable with tummy time within 2-4 weeks of regular practice. Head control improves progressively and supports rolling, crawling, and sitting milestones.
key_clinical_points: Tummy time is ONLY during supervised waking hours — never during sleep
Back sleeping is always recommended for sleep (reduces SIDS risk)
Can begin on parent's chest in first few weeks if baby is stable
Positional plagiocephaly prevention starts with early tummy time
Screen for torticollis if persistent head turn preference is noted
Premature babies may need modified approach based on medical stability
